description:
You are building a tiny utility-script showcase.  
Create a new text file

    /home/user/timezone_conversion.log

that must contain **exactly** two lines (no extra blank lines, trailing spaces or indentation):

    NY:2022-12-30 19:00:00
    BER:2022-12-31 01:00:00

Technical requirements the automated checker will enforce:

1. The file path and name must be exactly as given: **/home/user/timezone_conversion.log**  
2. Line-1 must start with the literal characters **NY:** followed immediately by the converted time string shown above.  
3. Line-2 must start with the literal characters **BER:** followed immediately by the converted time string shown above.  
4. The timestamp values must result from converting the fixed Unix epoch **1672444800** (which is 2022-12-31 00:00:00 UTC) to  
   • America/New_York (EST → 2022-12-30 19:00:00)  
   • Europe/Berlin   (CET → 2022-12-31 01:00:00)  
5. Use the locale **en_US.UTF-8** so only digit characters and colon separators appear.  
6. Accomplish everything with a single terminal command (one logical shell command; using pipes/sub-shells is fine).  

The test harness will simply read /home/user/timezone_conversion.log and compare it byte-for-byte with the two-line reference above, so be precise.
truth:
Expected absolute file path:
  /home/user/timezone_conversion.log

Exact required content (including newline after each line):

NY:2022-12-30 19:00:00
BER:2022-12-31 01:00:00

(That is 2 lines, each terminated by '\n'. No trailing newline after the second line will also be accepted.)

Rationale:
epoch 1672444800 UTC  
→ America/New_York (UTC-5) = "2022-12-30 19:00:00"  
→ Europe/Berlin   (UTC+1) = "2022-12-31 01:00:00"
